title: JSONL scanner and incremental indexer
description:
Implement the indexer package that scans ~/.claude/projects/ for session JSONL files, parses them, and syncs metadata into SQLite.

Refer to docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-13-ccs-design.md for JSONL format details.

Key context:
- CC sessions live at ~/.claude/projects/<sanitized-path>/<uuid>.jsonl
- Skip files inside 'subagents/' subdirectories
- Each JSONL line is a JSON object; key fields: sessionId, cwd, timestamp, gitBranch, slug (session name), type, message.role, message.content
- message.content can be a string OR an array of content blocks (extract text from type:'text' blocks)
- type field values include: 'user', 'assistant', 'file-history-snapshot', 'permission-mode' — only count user/assistant for message_count
- Incremental: compare file size + mod time to detect changes; only re-parse changed/new files
- Remove index entries for files that no longer exist on disk

This package depends on internal/model and internal/db which are created by task ccs-01-foundation.
requirements: Create internal/indexer/scanner.go with ScanSessions(claudeDir string) function that walks ~/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl, skips subagents/ dirs, returns list of discovered SessionFile structs (path, projectDir, sessionID, fileSize, fileModTime)

Create internal/indexer/parser.go with ParseSessionFile(path string) function that reads a JSONL file and extracts: sessionId, cwd, gitBranch, slug/name, first user message (truncated 200 chars), last message (truncated 200 chars), message count, first timestamp, last timestamp
Handle message.content being either a string or []interface{} array of content blocks — extract text from blocks with type 'text'

Create internal/indexer/indexer.go with Indexer struct that takes a *db.DB, runs ScanSessions, diffs against DB records (by file_size + file_mod_time), re-parses only changed/new files, upserts into DB, purges deleted sessions
Add Indexer.Reindex() method that forces full re-parse regardless of file_size/file_mod_time
Add tests in internal/indexer/parser_test.go that test JSONL parsing with sample data covering: normal sessions, sessions with array content blocks, sessions with missing fields, empty files
Verify the project compiles with 'go build ./cmd/ccs/' and all tests pass with 'go test ./...'
